subject: Q2 Ingredient Pricing — Need Commitment by Friday
body:
Hey Jordan,

Heads up — our gelatin & sweetener supplier is raising prices 12% starting April 1. I can lock in current pricing for your Q2 order but I need a commitment by this Friday (March 7).

Here's what I'm looking at for your usual volume:

- Gelatin (250 bloom): $4,200/ton → holds at $3,750/ton if committed by Friday
- Cane Sugar: $1,100/ton → holds at $950/ton
- Corn Syrup: $2,800/ton → holds at $2,500/ton

Your Q1 volume was 45 tons gelatin + 20 tons sugar. Want to match or increase for Q2?

Let me know ASAP. Once Friday passes I can't hold these numbers.

Steve

subject: PO #4471 — 50,000 units Tropical Blast gummies
body:
Jordan,

Purchase Order #4471 attached. Summary:

- Product: Tropical Blast Gummies 10-piece bag (your SKU TB-10-100ct)
- Quantity: 50,000 units (500 cases)
- Delivery: March 28 to our Denver distribution center
- Price: $2.40/unit per our MSA
- Payment: Net 30

This is our biggest single order yet — we're rolling into 340 new retail locations in Colorado and Oklahoma next month. Need to confirm you can hit the March 28 date. If there's any risk to that timeline, let me know now so I can adjust our retail launch.

Also — can you send updated QC certificates for the current batch? Two of our retail partners are asking.

Bob

subject: Gelatin shipment delayed — ETA pushed to March 10
body:
Jordan,

Bad news. The 200kg gelatin order (your PO IM-2026-0089) got held up at the port. Original ETA was March 5, now looking at March 10.

I know you've got production scheduled for the 8th. Options:

1. I can source 50kg from our secondary supplier (different grade, 240 bloom vs your usual 250) and overnight it. Gets there March 6. Cost bump is ~$800.
2. Wait for the full shipment March 10. Push your production 2 days.
3. I check if anyone in my network has 200kg of 250 bloom available for immediate ship. No guarantees.

What do you want me to do?

Steve

subject: Re: New SKU discussion — Sugar-free line
body:
Hey Jordan,

Following up on our call last week about the sugar-free line. My team ran the numbers and we think there's real demand here. The sugar-free gummy market is growing 23% YoY and none of the major national candy brands have a credible product yet.

Here's what we'd need:
- 3 flavors to start (suggest: Watermelon, Mango, Mixed Berry)
- 5g and 10g pack options
- Target retail price: $24.99 for 30ct
- First production run: 10,000 units per SKU (60,000 total)
- Timeline: ready for our Q3 retail push (July 1)

Can Acme Confections handle the formulation? Do you need to bring in a food scientist or do you have that in-house?

I'd like to get an LOI signed by end of March so we can lock in shelf space with our retail partners.

Bob

subject: QC Report for Batch #IM-2026-GEL-0223
body:
Jordan,

Attached is the internal QC report for your latest gelatin batch:

- Batch: IM-2026-GEL-0223
- Bloom Strength: 251
- Moisture Content: 11.2%
- pH: 5.4
- Heavy Metals: All below LOQ
- Pesticides: ND (full panel, 96 analytes)
- Microbial: Pass

This batch tested clean across the board. Let me know if you need anything else for your records.

Steve
signals: action_item:file QC report for compliance records

subject: Customer complaint — weight inconsistency in Blueberry SKU
body:
Jordan,

Got a complaint from one of our Denver retail partners (Green Leaf Market on Colfax). They're saying the Blueberry Bliss gummies from the last shipment (lot BLU-0215) are testing below label weight.

Their in-house scale showed 7.2g per pack vs the 10g label claim. That's 28% below claim. Colorado weights-and-measures rules allow +/- 15% variance.

I need:
1. Your internal QC results for lot BLU-0215
2. Third-party lab certificate if you have one
3. A plan for the remaining inventory from that lot

This is time-sensitive. If the state gets involved it's a problem for both of us. Can you look into this today?

Bob
signals: action_item:pull QC results for lot BLU-0215

subject: New organic sweetener samples — try these?
body:
Hey Jordan,

We just got in a new organic erythritol + monk fruit blend from a supplier out of Thailand. Way better mouthfeel than the stevia blend you've been using for your low-sugar line.

I'm sending you 5kg of samples next week. No charge — just want your team's feedback. If it works for your formulation, pricing would be about $18/kg vs the $22/kg you're paying for the stevia blend.

No rush on this one. Just thought of you when I tasted the samples.

Steve

subject: Payment for January invoice #AC-2026-0112
body:
Jordan,

Just a heads up — our AP team processed payment for invoice #AC-2026-0112 ($87,400) yesterday. Should hit your account in 2-3 business days.

Also, February invoice (#AC-2026-0215, $62,100) is approved and in the queue. Net 30 puts that at March 17.

We're current on everything. Let me know if your records don't match.

Bob

subject: Re: Q2 Ingredient Pricing — Need Commitment by Friday
body:
Jordan,

Just bumping this — Friday is tomorrow. If I don't hear back by 5pm ET I'll have to let the pricing expire.

I'd hate for you to get hit with the 12% increase. Your Q2 at the new rate would be ~$5,400 more than the locked price.

Call me if easier: (303) 555-0147

Steve

subject: Retail partner intro — Rocky Mountain Retail Group
body:
Hey Jordan,

I want to introduce you to Jake Martinez, the buyer at Rocky Mountain Retail Group. They're a 12-location chain in the Front Range and they've been asking if they can buy direct from you for their house brand.

I told them about your white-label capabilities and they're interested. This wouldn't conflict with our MSA since their house brand is a different SKU.

Looping Jake in on the next email. He's expecting to hear from you.

Bob
signals: introduction:Jake Martinez at Rocky Mountain Retail Group

subject: Shipment manifest #TR-2026-0891 — please confirm receipt
body:
Jordan,

Our logistics portal is showing shipment #TR-2026-0891 as delivered to your facility but you haven't confirmed receipt yet. It's been 3 days — the portal flags unconfirmed shipments after 72 hours.

Can you or your team confirm receipt today? The manifest covers:
- 25 tons Gelatin (batch IM-2026-GEL-0223)
- 10 tons Cane Sugar (batch IM-2026-SUG-0218)

Lot numbers are on the manifest. Let me know if anything doesn't match.

Steve

subject: LinkedIn post about Acme Confections — want to tag you?
body:
Jordan,

I'm putting together a LinkedIn post about our supply chain partners for National Candy Month. Want to highlight the quality of your operation.

Draft:
"Proud to work with manufacturers who take quality seriously. @AcmeConfections's facility runs 99.7% weight accuracy across 2M+ units this year. That's the kind of partner you want when your brand is on the label."

Okay to post? Want me to tag your company page or your personal profile?

Bob

subject: Fwd: Industry newsletter — Candy Industry Magazine March issue
body:
Thought you might find this interesting. The article on page 12 about production line efficiency is worth a read.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
Candy Industry Magazine — March 2026
In this issue: New production methods, state-by-state regulatory update, Q1 market pricing data...

subject: Out of office: March 3-5
body:
I'll be at the National Confectionery Trade Show in Las Vegas March 3-5. Limited email access. For urgent PO issues contact my ops manager Sarah at sarah@cpgbrandco.com.

Bob

subject: Flavoring recall notice — batch FL-0198 (DOES NOT affect your orders)
body:
Jordan,

Just want to get ahead of this — one of our flavoring batches (FL-0198, Strawberry Natural) was flagged in a voluntary recall for elevated lead levels. 

This batch was NOT shipped to Acme Confections. Your last strawberry flavoring order was batch FL-0185, which is clean. I'm attaching the QC certificate for FL-0185 so you have it on file.

No action needed on your end. Just wanted you to hear it from me first in case it hits industry news.

Steve
signals: compliance:flavoring recall FL-0198 does not affect us

subject: Q3 volume forecast — big numbers
body:
Jordan,

Wanted to give you early visibility on our Q3 forecast so you can plan production:

- Tropical Blast 10-piece: 75,000 units/month (up from 50K)
- Blueberry Bliss 10-piece: 40,000 units/month
- Watermelon Wave 5-piece: 30,000 units/month
- NEW Sugar-Free line (if we proceed): 20,000 units/month per SKU

Total: ~225,000 units/month if sugar-free goes through, ~145,000 without.

Can your facility handle that volume? If we need to discuss capacity expansion or a second production run per week, let's get on a call next week.

Bob
